description_long: This is a two-story urban bakery building with a flat roof and a prominent blue water tower mounted on top. The facade features a red lower section with a striped blue-and-white awning above the entrance, flanked by two large windows. The upper story is clad in light gray siding with two rectangular windows, and the building shows signs of weathering and minor damage. Utility poles with overhead wires border the sidewalk, and there are two colored mailboxes and a utility box near the entrance. The structure sits on a concrete sidewalk corner, with visible piping and a rear exhaust vent. The overall appearance is functional and slightly aged, typical of a small neighborhood commercial building.
description: A two-story urban bakery with a red storefront, blue-and-white striped awning, and a rooftop water tower, set on a concrete sidewalk corner with utility poles and mailboxes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red storefront, blue-and-white awning, entrance door, two upper windows, mailboxes, and the water tower on the roof.
description_view_1: Side view with the awning partially visible, utility pole in front, a side window, and a utility box near the entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain gray siding, rooftop water tower, and visible piping and exhaust vent.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view showing the back wall, water tower, and utility poles, with mailboxes and piping visible on the side.
attribute_storefront_signage: There is a rectangular, horizontally oriented sign above the awning on the front facade. The sign appears to be a light tan or beige color with no visible text or logo from the images provided. It is mounted directly above the entrance and below the upper windows.
attribute_awning_with_bakery_branding: The awning above the storefront is rectangular, made of fabric, and features alternating blue and white vertical stripes. There is no visible bakery branding, logo, or text on the awning.
attribute_architectural_style: The building exhibits a utilitarian, early 20th-century American commercial style with a flat roof, simple rectangular form, and minimal ornamentation. The presence of a rooftop water tower and exposed utility elements adds an industrial touch.
